TWO CONFLICTING NIST DATABASES — root cause of all errors on this cell. (1) NIST WebBook (Huber & Herzberg 1979 compilation): ωexe=5.63 cm⁻¹ — CORRECT source. (2) NIST CCCBDB (Irikura 2007 ab initio): ωexe=6.385–6.41 cm⁻¹ — WRONG source. Both are branded "NIST"; CCCBDB cites H&H in its footnotes, so models conflate the two and report CCCBDB numbers while citing "Huber-Herzberg". GT 5.602 is from a primary spectroscopic reference; NIST WebBook 5.63 also scores exact (within 1%). OBSERVED FAILURE MODES BY MODEL: the-clone-flash (both QC and no-QC): preview finds correct 5.602; validation synthesis overrides it to 6.39 with conf=HIGH citing "Huber-Herzberg and NIST WebBook" — synthesis hits CCCBDB and mislabels it, overriding the correct preview value. the-clone-claude (both QC and no-QC): returns 6.12 (CLOSE, 9.25% off) — a CCCBDB variant, possibly rounded or from a different ab initio set. sonar without QC: returns correct 5.60. sonar+gemini QC: QC overrides correct sonar value with 6.38 (CCCBDB). sonar-pro without QC: returns correct 5.602. sonar-pro+gemini QC: QC overrides correct value with 6.52 (CCCBDB). claude-sonnet/opus: returns correct 5.602 or 5.6 from parametric memory. KEY DIAGNOSTIC: if a model cites "Huber-Herzberg" but returns a value in 6.1–6.5 range, it is using NIST CCCBDB not NIST WebBook.
